channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Cortez Masto leads Nevada Democrats in renewed effort against nuclear waste dump
item.description: Nevada's Democratic U.S. senators and representatives in Congress reintroduced the Nuclear Waste Informed Consent Act on Wednesday, gearing up for possible renewed efforts to put a nuclear dump in the state.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with explosion of online porn
item.description: The Supreme Court weighed whether an explosion in online pornography requires repudiating the court's precedents concerning sexual content as the justices Wednesday heard arguments in a challenge to Texas’s age-verification law for porn websites.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump adviser says president-elect is exploring options to 'preserve' TikTok
item.description: Trump’s pick for national security adviser, Florida Rep. Mike Waltz, said during an interview on Wednesday that the president-elect is exploring options to “preserve” TikTok.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Speaker Johnson removes chair of powerful House Intelligence Committee
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson is removing the GOP chairman of the powerful House Intelligence Committee, who was a vocal supporter of assistance for Ukraine and held other views that put him at odds with President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump and Biden both claim credit for Gaza ceasefire deal
item.description: President Joe Biden and President-elect Donald Trump are both claiming credit for Israel and Hamas agreeing to a ceasefire deal in Gaza after the White House brought Trump’s Middle East envoy into negotiations that have dragged on for months.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Thompson, chair of Jan. 6 committee, has talked with White House about pardons
item.description: Rep. Bennie Thompson (D-Miss.), who headed Congress’s special investigation into the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol, said Tuesday that he has been in talks with the White House about presidential pardons.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump will begin his presidency in delicate position, poll finds
item.description: A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll shows that Americans' support for President-elect Donald Trump's top priorities is split, despite his claims of a mandate for his agenda.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Biden promised to turn the page on Trump. Now he's being replaced by him
item.description: Joe Biden staked his presidency on the idea that he was the best person to keep Donald Trump out of power.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: County Board of Supervisors votes to hold special election to fill District 1 seat
item.description: In a swift and unanimous vote, the County Board of Supervisors has agreed to a special election to fill the vacancy left behind by Nora Vargas.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Vara, Fox 5

item.title: U.S. attorneys for Michigan resign before Trump takes office
item.description: Both of Michigan's U.S. attorneys have announced they will resign from their posts before Donald Trump is sworn into office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth beats back blows from Democrats in blistering hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump's choice for Defense secretary, on Tuesday emerged largely unscathed from an at times blistering confirmation hearing before the Senate Armed Services Committee.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans pass bill to ban transgender athletes from girls’ sports
item.description: House Republicans passed a bill Tuesday to bar transgender student-athletes from competing in girls’ sports, putting the contentious issue front and center following an election cycle that saw it become a frequent target of Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Gov. Hochul wants police on every subway train overnight
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Police should patrol every New York City subway train during the overnight hours, Gov. Kathy Hochul proposed during her State of the State address Tuesday. The state will help pay for two police officers on every subway train from 9 p.m. to 5 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Gov. Kathy Hochul delivers State of the State address in NY
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – During her State of the State address, Gov. Kathy Hochul proposed free tuition for older college students, more police on New York City's subways overnight and tax relief for New Yorkers.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Johnson backs conditions on California fire aid
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is backing placing conditions on disaster assistance for California in the wake of the destructive wildfires blazing around the Los Angeles area, a position already drawing opposition from Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Schools brace for clash with immigration officials ahead of Trump term
item.description: K-12 schools across the U.S. are gearing up for potential efforts to shield their students as the incoming Trump administration sets its sights on deporting millions of people.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo raises $3.4 million in 2024 for re-election bid
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo is off to a strong start in building campaign funds, raising about $3.4 million over the past year, according to mandatory campaign filing reports.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams addresses migrants, crime and political future
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams had a wide-ranging press conference Monday discussing everything from closing down migrant shelters, to subway safety, to his tough reelection battle.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: What Project 2025 says about the first 180 days of a presidency
item.description: Project 2025 lists four pillars as part of its plan, and the fourth pillar is an unpublished 180-day "Transition Playbook.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Biden: Govt. to give $770 to each resident affected by LA wildfires
item.description: President Joe Biden convened senior officials to discuss the federal response to the LA County fires.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Biden announces student debt relief for 150,000 borrowers, bringing total helped to more than 5M
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it was forgiving student loans for more than 150,000 borrowers, bringing the total number of individuals impacted by debt relief in President Biden’s term to over 5 million.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's inauguration weekend features fireworks, MAGA rally, inaugural balls
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration weekend will start with a fireworks display outside his northern Virginia golf club and end with a trio of inaugural balls where he will speak, the committee planning the events announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Florida Gov. DeSantis open to special session for immigration, hurricane relief
item.description: It’s widely known that Gov. Ron DeSantis is open to the idea of a special session to help resolve the condo affordability crisis. However, the governor is also open to the idea of state lawmakers doing more before the yearly scheduled session starts.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA Mayor Bass stonewalls reporter for 90 seconds at airport
item.description: Video taken on an airport jetway shows Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass repeatedly ignoring questions from a reporter about her response to the wildfires ripping through the area this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5
